scope: hero.archer runtime integration (Neutral/Idle/Move/Attack v3.2) into the actual game runtime, actual-board evidence, x1 + x4 validation

PR81.role: 16-bit compatibility check, base of this runtime-integration branch
PR81.head: 5a42b921f3371bfdaf1c5f22e5bbf750b296818d

assetHashPreservation.method: SHA-256 re-hash of every protected binary, before and after all runtime code changes

attackV3_2Frame011ExactNeutralMasterMatc.note: exact match to Neutral Master, as approved

assetHashPreservation.attackV3_2AllTwelveFrames: all 12 frames (000-011) re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical to the approved package on record
assetHashPreservation.idlePackageEightFrames: all 8 frames re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical against data/design/archer-idle-package-exact-file-approval-v1.json
assetHashPreservation.movePackageEightFrames: all 8 frames re-hashed and confirmed byte-identical against data/design/archer-move-package-exact-file-approval-v1.json

implementationSummary.approach: additive, backward-compatible extension -- new per-file variable-duration animation code path (ARCHER_FRAME_SEQS / updateArcherAnim / loadArcherFrames), triggered only for cfg.sprite==='ArcherReal'. The pre-existing single-sheet fixed-duration UV-offset animation system (ASSET_META / updateAnim's WALK_SEQ/ATTACK_SEQ path) is completely untouched and used unmodified by all other 19 registered sprites.
implementationSummary.heroRosterChange: HERO_DEFS.archer.sprite changed from 'Sniper' (placeholder) to 'ArcherReal' -- the only HERO_DEFS edit in this task.
implementationSummary.testHook: window.__archerRuntimeTestHook (bottom of src/game.js) -- additive, not referenced by any gameplay code path, exposes existing internals (placeTestUnit/getUnits/setSpeedMul/setPhase/archerLoaded/startTestBattle/stepUnit/renderFrame) for automated Playwright evidence capture.
implementationSummary.facingFlipCorrection: An existing generic attack-time facing-flip mechanism (updateUnit()'s body.scale.x = target-relative -1/1) already applies to the Archer automatically -- no new code needed to satisfy the approved runtimeFlipX=true convention during Attack. This does not extend to Idle/Move (pre-existing characteristic shared by all sprite units, out of scope to change here).
implementationSummary.noProjectileSystemBuilt: No projectile/VFX spawn system was built for Release-frame synchronization -- none exists for any unit in this codebase, and building one is judged a new Core Combat feature, out of the smallest-safe-method scope for this task. Disclosed honestly, not fabricated.

actualTestsRun.toolchain: Playwright + headless Chromium, local static HTTP server serving the real worktree files, vendored THREE.js r128 (CDN network-blocked, same limitation as PR #81)
actualTestsRun.idleLoopDeterministic: PASS (all 8 frames reached in order, loop wraps correctly)
actualTestsRun.moveLoopDeterministic: PASS (archerSeqKey transitions to 'move', multiple distinct frames, anchor stays grounded)
actualTestsRun.attackFullDrawDeterministic: PASS (frame idx 7 at 633ms observed vs 630ms model)
actualTestsRun.attackReleaseDeterministic: PASS (frame idx 8 at 733ms observed vs 720ms model)
actualTestsRun.checkpoint2_playbackTransitionAnchorX4: PASS (groundedAnchorExact=true, noMapMissing=true, 240 real-rendered rAF samples, zero errors)
actualTestsRun.checkpoint3_releaseMarkerProjectileTimin: PASS (logical timing confirmed via deterministic trace; regression check confirmed Sniper/original sheet path unaffected; x4 real-rendered display-sampling result reported as a disclosed non-gating characteristic, see runtime-test-report.md)

x1VisualResult: PASS -- Idle/Move/Attack-Full-Draw/Attack-Release/Recovery/left-facing/right-facing all captured as real board screenshots, character identity clearly verifiable (green hood/hair, green-and-gold costume, elf ears, ornate bow), anchor/pivot correct with no feet/ground gap in any capture.

x4CheckpointResults.checkpoint3: PASS (with disclosed x4 display-sampling characteristic, not a functional defect -- see runtime-test-report.md)

markerProjectileEvidence.reason: No projectile/VFX spawn system exists for any unit in this codebase; release-frame timing verified directly instead (see release-marker-diagnostic.md)

limitations: THREE.js r128 itself could not be loaded from the real CDN in this environment (network-blocked); a vendored r128 copy was substituted, same mitigation as PR #81's compatibility check.
No projectile/VFX system exists for any unit; Release-frame synchronization is verified via direct frame-timing rather than a projectile-spawn event.

At real x4-speed rendering, the narrow Release-frame (idx 8, 70ms window) can be stepped over by the display's fixed 60fps refresh without being visually sampled in a given short window -- a disclosed, non-blocking characteristic of high-speed frame-based rendering, not a combat-correctness defect (damage timing is on an independent cooldown, unaffected).
Facing/flip is satisfied automatically during Attack via a pre-existing generic mechanism, but not during Idle/Move for any sprite-based unit (pre-existing, not Archer-specific).

Wave 1's 'Slime' enemy type has no ASSET_META sprite entry (pre-existing, unrelated to Archer) and renders as a placeholder box in both this test and real production; visible in one secondary evidence screenshot, disclosed in its caption.
Only Chromium/Blink was exercised; WebKit/Gecko were not tested in this environment (same disclosed limitation as PR #81).

(Added by repeatedAttackTimingReworkV1, SUPERSEDED by repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2 -- see next entry) Under sustained attack-speed buffs pushing the cooldown/cycle ratio well past the base ~1.78x, the cap=1 replay queue means not every individual attack event gets its own unique visual Full-Draw/Release acknowledgment -- damage and cooldown still apply exactly on schedule for every event regardless. This was believed to be non-binding at base stats; a re-audit found it is NOT, see the v2 entry below.

(Added by repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2) Because Attack v3.2's cycle (1.27s) is structurally slower than the base attack cooldown (0.7143s) -- attacks fire at 1.4/s but a full replay can complete at most 0.787/s -- no queue cap of any finite size can give every event its own dedicated FULL-REPLAY acknowledgment at base speed (proven mathematically and by trace, including a rejected cap=2 attempt). Fixed via a two-tier model: cap=1 full replay plus an instant self-flash for queue-saturated overflow, so every event still gets SOME visible acknowledgment (full replay or flash), proven exactly by trace. This closes the gap left by v1's limitation above.

regressionResults.nonArcherUnitTested: sniper (heroKey), sprite='Sniper', original ASSET_META/UV-offset sheet system
regressionResults.conclusion: the pre-existing sheet-based animation path for all other units is completely unaffected by this Archer-specific additive extension

rollbackInstructions: Revert the single commit on cc/archer-v3-2-runtime-integration-v1 that modifies src/game.js (HERO_DEFS.archer.sprite back to 'Sniper', remove ARCHER_FRAME_SEQS/updateArcherAnim/loadArcherFrames/__archerRuntimeTestHook additions and the makeUnit/updateAnim branch extensions). No asset binaries need any rollback action since none were modified. No other file outside src/game.js and the new docs/data-record paths listed above was touched.
nextHumanDecision: Human Pilot Acceptance Review of this Draft PR: inspect the actual-board screenshots and video, review runtime-test-report.md and release-marker-diagnostic.md, and decide whether to set pilotAccepted/canonicalApproved/finalRuntimeApproved/merged to true. This record intentionally does not self-approve.

repeatedAttackTimingReworkV1.auditSourceDefect: hero.archer basic-attack cooldown (71.43cs at base attack_speed 1.4) is faster than Attack v3.2's approved 127cs visual cycle; repeated attacks during sustained combat got no visual acknowledgment while one long cycle played through underneath them. Damage/cooldown/targeting/mana/lifesteal were never affected -- purely a visual gap.
repeatedAttackTimingReworkV1.rootCause: updateArcherAnim() never read u.animTimer, only u.archerFrameTimer/archerSeqKey; the original per-attack `u.animTimer=0` reset was inert for Archer because updateArcherAnim's reset-guard only trips on an archerSeqKey transition, which never re-fires while animState stays 'attack' across repeated triggers. No mid-cycle restart was actually happening prior to this fix.
repeatedAttackTimingReworkV1.fixSelected: capped (cap=1) natural-boundary replay queue -- archerPendingAcks, gated strictly on u.archerSeqs truthiness

alternativesRejectedAndWhy.doNothing: fails the requirement that repeated attacks get visual acknowledgment
alternativesRejectedAndWhy.restartOnEveryEvent: cooldown (71.43cs) lands ~1cs before Release begins (63cs); unconditional restart would suppress Release from ever being shown under sustained fire
alternativesRejectedAndWhy.unboundedQueue: calculated ~0.78 net extra attacks queued per cycle at base stats -> ~18 backed-up replay-cycles after ~30s of continuous combat -> ~23 extra seconds of visible post-combat attacking; rejected as unacceptable runtime risk

validationRun.validateArcherAttackV3_2ExactPackageAppr: exit 1, FAIL -- expected historical-validator commit-scope mismatch (its changed-path allowlist is scoped to PR #80's own commit range), not weakened/rewritten, not a regression

repeatedAttackTimingReworkV1.testHarnessDefectFoundAndFixedDuringThis: A synthetic partial-shape 'dummy' target object (first approach) crashed when the game's own concurrently-running animate() rAF loop tried to move/render it (missing .body etc.) -- fixed by using a real spawned enemy instead. A second contamination source was then found: real wall-clock time between separate page.evaluate() round trips let the concurrent animate() loop's dt-independent atkCooldown<=0 check fire before the deterministic loop's own first step -- fixed by running setup+trace+recovery inside one synchronous page.evaluate() call plus forcing speedMul=0. Both were test-harness issues, not src/game.js defects; disclosed here rather than silently discarding the first (wrong) result.

repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2.auditSourceDefect: Independent re-audit of v1: cap=1 replay queue silently dropped acknowledgment for some valid attack events even at base attack speed (audited: 6 attack events, only 4 visual cycles). Combat correctness (damage/cooldown/mana/lifesteal/targeting/protected-assets/regression) was reconfirmed PASS by the same re-audit -- this was purely a visual-completeness gap in v1's own fix.
repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2.structuralRootCause: Attack v3.2's cycle (D=1.27s) is structurally slower than the base attack cooldown (C=1/1.4=0.7143s): attack rate 1/C=1.400 events/s exceeds the max full-replay completion rate 1/D=0.787 cycles/s. Because arrivals permanently outpace departures, NO finite queue cap can give every event its own dedicated full-cycle replay under sustained fire -- at most C/D=~56% of events ever could, for any cap. Verified both mathematically and empirically (cap=2 was implemented, traced, and still lost events within an 8s sustained-fire window before being rejected).

designComparison.A_cap1_v1_current: rejected -- misses ~1 in 3 events under sustained fire (the audited defect)
designComparison.B_unboundedQueue: rejected -- arrival rate (1.4/s) permanently exceeds drain rate (0.787/s), so backlog grows without bound under sustained fire; same ~23s post-combat drift risk previously identified
designComparison.C_boundedQueueCapGreaterThan1: rejected -- tested at cap=2 and still lost events under sustained fire (proven by trace, not just reasoned); any larger fixed cap only delays saturation, never eliminates the structural gap, and increases worst-case post-combat drift proportionally
designComparison.D_coalescedAcknowledgment_selected: cap=1 full-replay queue kept (Full Draw/Release still shown in full whenever a slot is free) PLUS an instant self-flash (reusing the existing generic applyHitFlash() helper) for any event that arrives while the queue is already saturated -- gives literally every event some visible acknowledgment, touches no animation-frame state, zero new assets
designComparison.E_alternativeSyncMethod: out of scope -- would require changing frame timing/Full Draw/Release semantics to keep pace with cooldown, explicitly forbidden

repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2.fixSelected: two-tier acknowledgment: tier 1 = existing cap=1 natural-boundary replay queue (archerPendingAcks, unchanged from v1); tier 2 = applyHitFlash(u, 0xfff2a8, 70) self-flash on the archer for any event arriving while tier 1 is saturated. Both gated strictly on u.archerSeqs truthiness.
repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2.evidenceOnlyInstrumentationAdded: u.archerFlashAckCount -- an additive counter incremented at the flash call site, read by no gameplay code, added because a synchronous dt-driven test harness cannot reliably observe setTimeout-based flash reversion (the browser's real clock does not advance between steps inside one synchronous page.evaluate() call).
repeatedAttackTimingReworkV2.reportingCorrection: SUPERSEDED by repeatedAttackTimingReworkV3: the 'completedReplays: 7' figure above folded a still-in-progress replay at the measurement window's edge into the completed count -- v3 corrects this to completedReplays=6 + inProgressReplayAtWindowClose=1 (same underlying trace, precise categorization). The tail-drift figure here was also an ESTIMATE ('<=1.27s'); v3 measures it directly (2267ms).

auditSourceBlockers.blocker1_visualFeedbackOwnershipConflict: v2's overflow-acknowledgment tier reused applyHitFlash(), sharing its timer (hitFlashTimer) and body.material.color with every unit's incoming-damage feedback. An attack acknowledgment and a hit-taken flash firing near each other could clobber one another's timer/color.
auditSourceBlockers.blocker2_archerStateNotResetOnWaveTransi: resetForWave() (the real function healPlayerTeam() calls for every placed unit) never cleared archerPendingAcks/archerSeqKey/archerFrameTimer/archerFrameIdx/the evidence-only archerFlashAckCount counter, or any new visual-acknowledgment channel, between waves.

repeatedAttackTimingReworkV3.combatCorrectnessReconfirmed: damage/cooldown/mana/lifesteal/targeting/protected-assets/non-Archer-regression were all reconfirmed unaffected by the same re-audit -- both blockers are visual-feedback and state-lifecycle issues only.

fix1_ownershipSeparation.design: a fully separate Archer-only visual channel: archerAckGlow (standalone glow mesh, created only for cfg.sprite==='ArcherReal'), its own material color/opacity, its own timer field (archerAckFlashTimer). applyArcherAckFlash()/restoreArcherAckGlow() mirror applyHitFlash()/restoreBodyColor()'s shape but never read or write body.material.color or hitFlashTimer.
fix1_ownershipSeparation.hitFlashUntouched: applyHitFlash/restoreBodyColor/hitFlashTimer/body.material.color are exactly the same code used for every unit's damage-taken feedback -- unmodified.
fix1_ownershipSeparation.disposal: archerAckGlow disposed for free by disposeObjectTree(u.group) (a normal child mesh); its own pending timer explicitly cleared in disposeUnitVisual(), mirroring the existing hitFlashTimer clear.
fix1_ownershipSeparation.proofMethod: real setTimeout timers (not stepUnit's synchronous dt) -- the whole point is genuine real-time channel independence

fix2_waveResetImplementation.design: Archer-gated block added to resetForWave() (gated on u.archerSeqs, no-op for every other unit) resetting archerPendingAcks/archerSeqKey/archerFrameTimer/archerFrameIdx/archerFlashAckCount to exactly makeUnit()'s fresh-unit values, plus restoreArcherAckGlow(u).
fix2_waveResetImplementation.proofMethod: REAL production path: real spawnWave() -> deliberately-dirtied archer state (confirmed genuinely dirty, not assumed) -> real onWaveCleared() (the exact function animate() calls once eAlive===0) -> a real click on the actual #resultBtn DOM element -> real healPlayerTeam()/resetForWave() -> archer state read -> Wave B started for real -> one real deterministic Attack step taken.
fix2_waveResetImplementation.testHarnessGapClosed: placeTestUnit() alone doesn't register into placedUnits[] (only the shop's moveUnitTo() does, in real gameplay) -- a one-line registerAsPlaced() test hook was added so the test archer goes through the exact same reset a genuinely shop-placed hero gets. Test-hook addition only, not a change to any reset logic.

reportingCorrections.replayAccountingCategories: completedReplay (finished within the window) / inProgressReplay (still playing at window close, at most 1) / queuedReplay (sitting in archerPendingAcks at window close, at most 1) / overflowAcknowledgment (flash-tier) -- proven to sum exactly to the attack-event count
reportingCorrections.measurementMethod: measured directly from target-death instant to the exact simulated-time offset at which animState==='idle' and archerPendingAcks===0 -- not estimated; taken from the x1 trace's own recovery phase using that trace's genuinely-worst-case leftover state (1 in-progress + 1 queued replay), not a synthetic worst case.
